questions.text: A line segment joining a vertex of a triangle to the mid-point of its opposite side is a:
questions.explanation: A median connects a vertex to the center of the opposite side; a triangle has three medians.

questions.text: The perpendicular line segment from a vertex of a triangle to its opposite side is the:
questions.explanation: An altitude represents the height of the triangle from a specific base.

questions.text: An exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of its:
questions.explanation: The Exterior Angle Property states that an exterior angle equals the sum of its remote interior angles.

questions.text: The sum of the three angles of a triangle is always:
questions.explanation: This is known as the Angle Sum Property of a triangle.

questions.text: A triangle in which two sides are of equal length is called:
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the angles opposite the equal sides are also equal.

questions.text: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite to the right angle is the:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is the longest side in a right-angled triangle.

questions.text: According to Pythagoras property, in a right triangle:
questions.explanation: The square on the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares on the other two sides.

questions.text: The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side.
questions.explanation: This property ensures that a closed triangle can actually be formed.

questions.text: Each angle of an equilateral triangle measures:
questions.explanation: Since all sides are equal, all angles are equal: 180° / 3 = 60°.

questions.text: Can a triangle have two right angles?
questions.explanation: If two angles were 90°, their sum would be 180°, leaving 0° for the third angle, which is impossible.
